activities_projects.title: Thermal Decomposition of Ferrous Sulphate
activities_projects.objective: Observe the breakdown of a single reactant into multiple products using heat
steps: Note the initial green color of the ferrous sulphate crystals
Heat the crystals in a dry boiling tube over a flame
Observe the change in color of the residue
Carefully smell the gas evolved by wafting it toward your nose
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the characteristic smell of burning sulphur and the color change from green to reddish-brown ($Fe_2O_3$)
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct chemical equation and identification of the three products formed (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Electrolysis of Water
activities_projects.objective: Demonstrate decomposition of water using electrical energy
steps: Set up the electrodes in the mug and fill with water until electrodes are immersed
Add a few drops of acid to make water conducting
Invert two test tubes filled with water over the electrodes
Connect to the battery and compare the volume of gas collected in both tubes
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that the volume of Hydrogen gas is double the volume of Oxygen gas ($H_2O \rightarrow H_2 + O_2$)
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of the 2:1 ratio of gases based on the molecular formula of water (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Silver Chloride Photo-Decomposition
activities_projects.objective: Observe how light energy can cause a chemical reaction
steps: Place about 2g of white silver chloride in a watch glass
Place the watch glass in sunlight for some time
Observe the color change from white to grey
Analyze why the change occurred
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes photolytic decomposition and its application in black and white photography
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate description of the decomposition of $AgCl$ into Silver metal and Chlorine gas (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Investigating Exothermic & Endothermic Reactions
activities_projects.objective: Measure temperature changes to classify energy flow
steps: Mix barium hydroxide and ammonium chloride in a beaker
Stir the mixture with a glass rod
Touch the bottom of the beaker and record the temperature
Compare this with the reaction between water and quicklime
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates between reactions that absorb energy (endothermic) and those that release it
activities_projects.evaluation: Data table showing temperature drop/rise for both reactions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Displacement Competition: Metal Strips
activities_projects.objective: Compare the reactivity of different metals
materials: Copper sulphate and Zinc sulphate solutions
steps: Place an iron nail in copper sulphate and a copper wire in ferrous sulphate
Place a zinc strip in copper sulphate
Observe which reactions show a color change or coating
Rank the metals (Zn, Fe, Cu) in order of reactivity
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Constructs a mini-reactivity series based on experimental evidence
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct reasoning for why some reactions did not occur (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Redox Reaction: Heating Copper
steps: Heat copper powder in a china dish until it turns black
Identify the black substance formed (Copper Oxide)
Pass Hydrogen gas over the heated black material
Observe if the original brown color returns
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands how oxygen gain (oxidation) and oxygen loss (reduction) occur
activities_projects.evaluation: Identifying the oxidizing and reducing agents in the reaction (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Precipitation Hunt
activities_projects.objective: Identify insoluble salts formed in double displacement
steps: Mix 3ml of sodium sulphate solution with 3ml of barium chloride solution
Observe the formation of a white insoluble substance
Write the ionic equation for the reaction
Filter the mixture to isolate the precipitate
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Defines a precipitation reaction and identifies the insoluble product
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of Barium Sulphate as the precipitate (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Science of Rust Prevention
activities_projects.objective: Test the effectiveness of different anti-corrosion methods
steps: Subject four nails to different conditions: (1) Dry air, (2) Salt water, (3) Oiled in water, (4) Painted in water
Observe over 3-5 days
Record the degree of rusting in each tube
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates how barrier protection prevents the oxidation of iron
activities_projects.evaluation: Comparative report on the most and least effective prevention methods (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Equation Balancing: Conservation of Mass
activities_projects.objective: Mathematically prove the law of conservation of mass
steps: Take the balanced equation: $CH_4 + 2O_2 \rightarrow CO_2 + 2H_2O$
Calculate the total molecular mass of the reactants
Calculate the total molecular mass of the products
Compare the two values
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Verifies that total mass remains constant in a chemical reaction
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of mass calculations for both sides of the equation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Antioxidants vs. Rancidity
activities_projects.objective: Observe the effect of air on fats and oils
steps: Leave one set of chips in an open jar and another in a sealed jar with an oxygen absorber (or nitrogen flushed if possible)
Wait for 48 hours
Observe the change in smell and taste (rancidity)
Research common food antioxidants like BHA or BHT
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands how oxidation affects food quality and the role of antioxidants
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of the observation log and research on food preservatives (10 marks)
